channel.title: esp32 on Home Smart Mesh
channel.description: Recent content in esp32 on Home Smart Mesh

item.title: Datasheets
item.description: ESP32 # Datasheet # Datasheet Blockdiagram # Memory Map # Power Modes # WROOM Datasheet # ESP32-WROOM-32 Datasheet Pin Layout # ESP32-S2 # Datasheet # ESP32-S2 Datasheet Block Diagram # Memory # Power Modes # S2 Technical Reference Manual # TRM

item.title: Debug
item.description: PlatformIO # ESP-Prog Pinout # Connection # Official Guide # Espressif - ESP Prog Guide

item.title: Software
item.description: Arduino ESP32 # platform package as an idf component ESP-IDF # esp-idf esp-iot-solution mesh-dev-framework mdf prog guide Over The Air Update # >get_idf >idf.py set-target esp32 OTA Github Readme >idf.py menuconfig https://10.0.0.36:8070/hello-world.bin >cp ca_cert.pem ../simple_ota_example/server_certs/ >idf.py -p /dev/ttyUSB0 flash monitor on another linux host, copied ca_cert.pem then tested : >curl -v https://10.0.0.36:8070/simple_ota.bin --cacert ca_cert.pem > simple_ota.

item.title: Fisheye Cam OV3660
item.description: 3 Mega Pixels OV3660 Fisheye lens DFOV 120° Ultra low power, sleep current 2μA 270mAh battery PSRAM 8MB Product page # Fisheye Timer camera $20 Platformio porting # board definition # local file : boards\m5stack-timer-cam.json should be added to the platformio/platform-espressif32 repo still missing from v2.1.0 pins declaration # file required pins_arduino.h available on pins_arduino.h@1.0.5 not yet released Applications # wakeup # m5stack/Arduino wakeup example sep32home/Platformio wakeup example Build info PACKAGES: - framework-arduinoespressif32 0.

item.title: TTGO T-Display
item.description: Simple Example # t-display_controller dependencies # TFT_eSPI_ttgo_t-display fork from Bodmer/TFT_eSPI GfxUi lib_deps = https://github.com/ESP32Home/TFT_eSPI_ttgo_t-display.git#2.3.4_t-display https://github.com/ESP32Home/GfxUi.git#v1.0.1 Example Code # click to expand the example code... #include <Arduino.h>#include <TFT_eSPI.h>#include <SPI.h>#include "GfxUi.h"#include <SPIFFS.h> TFT_eSPI tft = TFT_eSPI(135, 240); GfxUi ui = GfxUi(&tft); void setup() { Serial.begin(115200); tft.init(); tft.setRotation(3); SPIFFS.begin(true);//FORMAT_SPIFFS_IF_FAILED ui.drawBmp("/esp_home.bmp", 0, 0);//'ui' needs tft and SPIFF tft.setCursor(0, 0); tft.

item.title: TTGO T5-EPaper
item.description: Simple example # epaper-TTGO-T5_1_2-hello dependencies # GxEPD lib_ldf_mode = deep+ lib_deps =GxEPD@3.1.0 Example # #include <SPIFFS.h>#include <epaper.h> EPaper epaper; void setup() { Serial.begin(115200); SPIFFS.begin(); epaper.init(); epaper.display.fillScreen(GxEPD_WHITE); epaper.drawBitmap("/esp_home.bmp", 0, 50, true); epaper.displayText("Hello simple", 20, EPaper::RIGHT_ALIGNMENT); epaper.display.update(); } void loop() { } Display # GxGDEW029Z10 2,9 Zoll White / Black / Red 296x128 References # LilyGo epaper ESP-IDF repo LilyGO/ESP32_T5Epaper_2.9inch fork from loboris/ESP32_ePaper_example LilyGo epaper platformio-arduino repo Xinyuan-LilyGO/T5-Ink-Screen-Series fork from TTGO-EPaper-Series Waveshare specification pdf 2.

item.title: M5 Stick Plus
item.description: 6-Axis IMU IR transmitter Microphone RTC LCD (1.14 inch) Lipo Battery Passive Buzzer Product page # m5stack M5StickC Plus $14 Display # 1.14 inch 135x240 Color TFT LCD ST7789v2

item.title: ESP-Now Wrapper
item.description: Relates to # ESP-Mesh Wrapper Github repo # Github repo Status # Not satisfactory, as it is not reliable. The ESP-Now does not have a quality of service concept that ensures transmission like TCP, given the broadcast nature of the protocol. Also the handling of a wifi gateway does not allow a large enough listening time, so a wifi gateway is not possible and a serial one has to be developer.

item.title: ESP-Mesh Wrapper
item.description: Relates to # ESP-Now Wrapper Mesh Alternatives # Thread Zigbee Simple Mesh Github repo # Github repo Usage # flash the data file system containing config files config.json and the passwords secret.json with the command >pio run -t uploadfs compile and flash >pio run >pio run -t upload It’s enough repeat for multiple ESP boards and watch the serial output.
